page.title: overactive bladder — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for overactive bladder: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for overactive bladder: 116 alterations and 516 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Higher intakes of vitamin D, protein and potassium are significantly associated with decreased risks of onset of overactive bladder.
. The use of Lactobacillus-containing feminine products was associated with improved vaginal ecosystem and urogenital health.
A pilot study on the potential of photobiomodulation to safely modify symptoms of an overactive bladder.
Acupuncture has significant improvements in bladder capacity, urgency, frequency, and quality-of-life scores in women with overactive bladder.
Association between proton pump inhibitor use and overactive bladder risk in adults.
Cucurbita pepo-Rhus aromatica-Humulus lupulus combination reduces overactive bladder symptoms in women.
Efficacy and safety of a new complementary and alternative medicine (CAM) versus Solifenacin Succinate in women with overactive bladder syndrome.
Efficacy of cinnamon patch treatment for alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.
Electrical stimulation results in subjective improvement in overactive bladder.
Electroacupuncture for post-stroke overactive bladder.
Endocrine disruptors and bladder function: the role of phthalates in overactive bladder.
Extracorporeal magnetic stimulation has a beneficial effect on women with overactive bladder.
Foot reflexology is therapeutic for patients with symptomatic idiopathic detrusor overactivity.
Gosha-jinki-gan (GJG), a traditional Chinese medicine, may have therapeutic action in overactive bladder
LLLT may be clinically useful and safe for the treatment of overactive bladder.
PS-TENS is an effective and safe treatment option for children with overactive bladder.
Pumpkin seed oil extracted from C. maxima has the potential for prevention or treatment of urinary disorders.
THC/CBD oromucosal spray has shown to be effective in improving overactive bladder symptoms in multiple sclerosis patients.
The combination of TENS and mirabegron was more effective than mirabegron alone in the treatment of female patients with OAB.
The Japanese multi-herbal formual known as gosha-jinki-gan could be a safe and effective potential therapeutic alternative in females with overactive bladder.
The management of children and adolescents with overactive bladder refractory to treatment with parasacral transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation.
Transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation use in children with incontinence refractory to pharmacotherapy is safe and well tolerated.

Vaginal estriol therapy significantly reduces symptoms of urinary urgency, has a high rate of patient satisfaction, and can be used to treat postmenopausal women for at least 6 months after a tension-free vaginal tape procedure
"Green tea catechins decrease oxidative stress in surgical menopause-induced overactive bladder in a rat model."
Catechins prevents experimentally-induced hyperactive bladder in rats.
Hydrogen water could ameliorate bladder dysfunction secondary to bladder outlet obstruction by attenuating oxidative stress.
Resveratrol treatment could be an option to prevent obesity-associated overactive bladder.
Soy isoflavone replacement may be useful to treat or prevent overactive bladder.
Vinpocetine ameliorates metabolic-syndrome-associated bladder overactivity.

Saw palmetto extract at clinically relevant doses may exert a direct effect on the pharmacological receptors in the lower urinary tract, thereby improving urinary dysfunction in patients with benign prostatic hyperplasia and an overactive bladder.
The aim of this systematic review is to assess the efficiency and safety of acupuncture for adults with overactive bladder.
Sumac (R. aromatica) and goldenrod (S. virgaurea) inhibit muscarinic receptors in the bladder, indicating its potential therapeutic value in the treatment of overactive bladder syndrome.
